claim: Price per intelligent agent-hour, not price per token, is the correct metric for evaluating frontier models in agentic work
actionable: When deciding whether to upgrade to a more expensive model, calculate time saved per task multiplied by task value, not raw token cost per completion.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: Frontier models like Fable 5 are orchestrators, not workers—they shine when delegating to sub-agents rather than executing tasks directly
actionable: Reserve expensive models for orchestration and planning; use cheaper models for individual task execution within the orchestrated workflow.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: 80% of engineers don't need frontier models because task complexity is the primary driver of model necessity, not model capability alone
actionable: Before upgrading models, audit your task complexity; if most tasks are routine, invest in better prompts and task specification rather than better models.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: Multi-agent orchestration with frontier models beats single-agent approaches on both performance and latency, even with higher token costs
actionable: When facing complex tasks, structure workflows with one frontier orchestrator model controlling multiple specialized sub-agents rather than using one model for everything.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: Writing comprehensive specs (100+ lines, with images, structure) enables frontier models to produce one-shot solutions with minimal iteration
actionable: For agentic tasks, invest time in detailed specifications with examples and validation criteria before prompting; this reduces iteration and token waste.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: The progression of engineer capability with agents is: base agent → better prompting → more agents → custom agents → orchestration
actionable: Follow this progression systematically; don't jump to orchestration without mastering earlier stages, as orchestration compounds earlier mistakes.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: Scaling compute (more agents) with a frontier orchestrator improves accuracy and latency in multi-agent benchmarks, not just accuracy
actionable: When optimizing multi-agent systems, measure both accuracy and latency; more agents can improve both if orchestration is sound.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: Principal engineers delegate to scale; frontier models perform best as principal-level orchestrators, not as hands-on workers
actionable: Structure agentic workflows where the best model does strategic delegation and oversight while cheaper models execute individual tasks.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: The trade-off triangle for agents is performance, speed, and cost—with north-star being performance, then dynamically trading speed and cost
actionable: When configuring agents, prioritize getting correct results first, then optimize for speed or cost based on deployment context.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: Planning then reviewing is more efficient than back-and-forth prompting—frontier models excel at following detailed plans with minimal iteration
actionable: For agentic work, spend time upfront on comprehensive planning documents; let the model execute the plan with fewer interruptions and clarifications.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED

claim: Zero-touch engineering (shipping to production from a single prompt) is the north-star goal; requires planning, testing, review, and documentation automation
actionable: Build toward fully automated CI/CD pipelines where agents can plan, code, test, document, and deploy with minimal human intervention.
source_title: Claude Fable 5 BANNED: The First Model Agentic Engineers DON'T NEED
